metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the test-sweep to also rename `test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes` → e.g. `test_config_defaults_and_60_minutes`, update the section comment at line 189, and update the module docstring at line 7 to remove 'default-OFF' framing.
findings.finding: The plan's 'complete sweep' for test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py names only the assertion at line 192, but three additional stale 'default-OFF' artifacts in that same file are left unaddressed: the module docstring at line 7 ('the default-OFF config rollout'), the section comment at line 189 ('# ── config keys (default-OFF rollout + the back-out) ──'), and the test function name at line 190 ('test_config_defaults_off_and_60_minutes'). After the flip these will contradict the new True default.
findings.location: tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py — lines 7, 189, 190 (unenumerated stale text); What section 'Update stale default assertions in tests'
findings.suggested_fix: In test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py: rename the function to `test_config_defaults_and_60_minutes`, change the section comment to `# ── config keys (defaults + the back-out) ──`, and update the module docstring to `'the default-ON config rollout (operator-authorized 2026-07-11), and the byte-identical full-review back-out.'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] When flipping the assertion at test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166, also update the inline comment from `# inert by default (the evidence gate)` to e.g. `# active by default (operator-authorized 2026-07-11; explicit False is the back-out)`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC for test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166 updates only the assertion (`is False` → `is True`) but leaves the trailing inline comment on that same line — `# inert by default (the evidence gate)` — stale. After the flip the comment directly contradicts the new default.
findings.location: tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166 — inline comment (unenumerated); AC bullet 'tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166 asserts novelty_drop_active is True'
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 166 to: `assert vc.novelty_drop_active is True  # active by default (operator-authorized 2026-07-11; explicit False restores inert behavior)`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:71` to the doc-update sweep (amend 'inert by default' to reflect that novelty and remediation floors are now ON by default, with explicit-false as the back-out), and add `docs/plan-review-gate.md:363` to the listed line range for that file.
findings.finding: The plan's 'complete sweep' doc-update list omits two stale 'inert by default' phrases that will become factually incorrect after the flip: (a) `docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:71` — 'They compose; each is separately gated and inert by default' — ADR 0002 is entirely absent from the plan's update scope; (b) `docs/plan-review-gate.md:363` — 'each along an independent axis and inert by default' — this file is in scope but only lines 322-338 and ~370-374 are listed, leaving line 363 uncovered. After the flip, two of the three floors are no longer inert by default, making both phrases stale.
findings.location: What section / doc-update sweep list; docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:71; docs/plan-review-gate.md:363
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:71 change 'each is separately gated and inert by default' to 'each is separately gated (novelty + remediation floors default ON as of 2026-07-11 operator authorization; completion floor remains inert by default)'. In docs/plan-review-gate.md:363 apply the same scoped correction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC naming at least one behavioral test (or a short integration invocation) that exercises the rising floor and the remediation gate firing under a *default* VerifyConfig() (no explicit flag override), confirming the floor/gate produces the intended outcome — not merely that the config field reads True.
findings.finding: The default-flip to active behavior (remediation_mode=True, novelty_drop_active=True) has no acceptance criterion that exercises the new default code path end-to-end in a production-like run. The two cited test assertions (test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166, test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:192) only check that the *config default value* is True — they do not verify that the rising floor or remediation gate actually fires and produces the intended effect under default config. The plan's own 'Why' section states the machinery has been INERT behind default-False flags, meaning the default path has never been active before; flipping it is a behavior cutover that needs at least one behavioral AC proving the floor/gate activates correctly under the new defaults.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (full list); plan section 'Why'; test citations at test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:166 and test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py:192
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py includes a test that instantiates VerifyConfig() with no arguments and asserts _maybe_apply_rising_floor suppresses/floors a qualifying finding (i.e., the floor is active by default, not just configured True).' Mirror for the remediation gate. If such tests already exist, name them explicitly in the AC so the agent can confirm they pass.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete grep command to the sweep AC (or as a separate verification step) that confirms no stale default-OFF phrasing survives, e.g.: `grep -rn 'off by default\|Default False\|default-OFF\|default OFF' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ src/rebar/llm/code_review/ src/rebar/_config_schema.py` should return zero matches for the relevant config fields.
findings.finding: The 'complete sweep' acceptance criterion ('Every source location in the complete sweep list above carries default-ON framing with explicit-false named as the back-out') names 7+ file/line targets but provides no concrete grep or diff command to prove all locations were updated. The plan says 'verified by grep' in the What section but never specifies the pattern, making this AC's proving command implicit.
findings.location: AC: 'Every source location in the complete sweep list above (…) carries default-ON framing with explicit-false named as the back-out'
findings.suggested_fix: grep -rn -E '(off by default|Default False|default-OFF|default OFF)' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py src/rebar/_config_schema.py | grep -i 'novelty_drop\|remediation_mode' should produce no output after the sweep.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC3 by naming the specific test files/functions (or a targeted `pytest` invocation) that verify the explicit-False back-out path, so the proving command is concrete rather than relying on the implicit coverage of `make test`.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Setting either key to false in config restores byte-identical full-review behavior') is a behavioral claim with no concrete proving command. The parenthetical '(the off-path/triple-gate tests, which pass explicit False configs, run unmodified in behavior)' names a class of tests but not specific test names or a command, making it unverifiable as written.
findings.location: AC: 'Setting either key to false in config restores byte-identical full-review behavior (the off-path/triple-gate tests, which pass explicit False configs, run unmodified in behavior).'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the parenthetical with: '(verified by: pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py -k "explicit_false or backout or no_floor" — all off-path tests must pass without modification to their explicit False fixture configs).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure to the plan (or a pointer to the operator runbook): specify the config key path (e.g., `verify.novelty_drop_active = false` / `verify.remediation_mode = false`), the config file location, and how to apply it — not just the outcome.
findings.finding: The rollback/back-out mechanism is named ('explicit false is the back-out') but no explicit rollback procedure is specified — T4 requires more than acknowledgement; it requires a concrete reversibility mechanism (e.g., which config key, in which file, how to apply it in production) so an operator can actually execute the rollback.
findings.location: What section — comment-rewrite instructions and AC item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection to the What section, e.g.: 'Rollback: set `verify.remediation_mode = false` and/or `verify.novelty_drop_active = false` in the site config (typically `rebar.toml` or the deployment-specific override file) and restart the gate process. Both flags are independent; either can be reverted without affecting the other. Note: the follow-up task 49ae-258b-4534-4d83 will remove these flags; rollback must occur before that task ships.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand both ADR dated notes (0008 and 0037) to explicitly state that `novelty_drop_active` is a shared flag controlling both the plan-review rising floor and the code-review region-gated floor, and add a reciprocal cross-reference between the two ADRs.
findings.finding: The AC for ADR updates requires only 'a dated defaults-flipped note' without mandating that either note cross-reference the shared-gate coupling — a maintainer reading ADR 0008 (plan-review) or ADR 0037 (code-review) in isolation will not learn that `novelty_drop_active` governs both gates simultaneously, creating a hidden footgun when tuning or reverting one gate.
findings.location: AC lines: 'docs/adr/0008 and docs/adr/0037 carry a dated defaults-flipped note'; What section 'SHARED GATE' prose
findings.suggested_fix: In each ADR's dated note, add: 'Note: `verify.novelty_drop_active` is a shared gate; setting it false in config suppresses both this floor and the [plan-review / code-review] floor described in ADR [0008 / 0037]. See ADR [0037 / 0008] for the other consumer.' Mirror this cross-reference in docs/plan-review-gate.md and docs/review-kernel.md under the flag description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a staged rollout path (e.g. canary percentage or a phased config push) and document whether the explicit-false rollback requires a redeployment or is hot-reloadable, so operators know the recovery time window.
findings.finding: The plan flips two defaults that affect 100% of traffic simultaneously (both plan-review and code-review gates activate at once) with no staged rollout or canary step specified. While a flag-based rollback exists ('explicit false is the back-out'), the plan does not specify how rapidly that rollback can be applied (hot-reload vs. redeploy), nor is there a tested rollback procedure referenced.
findings.location: ## What — 'SHARED GATE — intentional cross-gate effect' paragraph; ## Why — field evidence paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollout sequence' subsection: (1) ship the code change with defaults flipped, (2) push config to a canary pool (e.g. 5-10% of runs) for one monitoring period, (3) promote to full traffic if no regression. For rollback: note whether rebar hot-reloads VerifyConfig or requires a process restart, and add a one-liner rollback command (e.g. `rebar config set verify.remediation_mode false verify.novelty_drop_active false`) to the ADR notes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:710` to the 'complete sweep' source list and update its comment to default-ON framing (e.g., 'ON by default; explicit false is the back-out') so the sweep is complete as claimed.
findings.finding: The plan's 'complete sweep, verified by grep' of source locations to update for default-ON framing omits gate_dispatch.py:710, which contains the stale comment 'Gated on verify.novelty_drop_active + self-gates inert with no prior memory' — implying inert/off-by-default behavior that will be inconsistent with the new default-ON state.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a bullet: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:710` — update the inline comment from 'Gated on verify.novelty_drop_active + self-gates inert with no prior memory' to 'Gated on verify.novelty_drop_active (ON by default; explicit false is the back-out) + self-gates inert with no prior memory'. Also add this location to the corresponding AC item.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
